selectWhen: The main target is HUD, menu, layout, readability, flow, or player-facing interaction presentation.
The user needs guidance on which UI evidence to gather or which adjacent specialist should own the next step.
avoidWhen: The main issue is animation controller or clip behavior.
The main issue is sound design or gameplay rule ownership.

responsibilities: Keep advice anchored to interface clarity and interaction flow.
Escalate ownership when the real issue is not presentation.
mustNot: Claim runtime-backed MCP specialist resources in the first slice.
Pretend UI owns gameplay or audio semantics.
